evals.name: single-page-conversion-smoke
evals.prompt: Use markdown.new to convert https://example.com into Markdown and show me the safest way to get JSON back instead of plain text.
evals.expected_output: Routes to the single-page conversion guidance, uses either GET with ?format=json or POST / with a JSON body, and points to references/url-conversion.md for the exact request patterns.

assertions.text: Mentions GET path-prefix conversion and the JSON mode for single-page conversion
Routes to references/url-conversion.md

evals.name: blob-vs-raw-edge-case
evals.prompt: I tried markdown.new on a GitHub blob URL and got garbage HTML. What should I change?
evals.expected_output: Explains that GitHub blob URLs are page views, recommends switching to the raw.githubusercontent.com URL, and points to the relevant gotcha and file conversion guidance.

assertions.text: Explains the GitHub blob vs raw distinction
Routes to references/gotchas.md or references/file-conversion.md

evals.name: crawl-disclosure
evals.prompt: Use markdown.new to crawl this docs section and give me one Markdown file I can download later. I also want to know how to poll the job status.
evals.expected_output: Routes to references/crawl.md, uses POST /crawl to start the job, explains the status endpoint, and mentions that default status output is combined Markdown while ?format=json returns structured records.

assertions.text: Routes to references/crawl.md
Mentions both crawl start and crawl status endpoints

evals.name: query-string-url-edge-case
evals.prompt: The source URL I need to convert already has query params and markdown.new keeps breaking when I stuff it into the path. What is the safer request shape?
evals.expected_output: Recommends POST / with a JSON body instead of GET path-prefix mode, explains that nested query strings are brittle, and routes to references/url-conversion.md plus references/gotchas.md.

assertions.text: Recommends POST / for URLs with their own query strings
Surfaces the query-string gotcha

evals.name: negative-local-markdown-editing
evals.prompt: Please clean up this local README.md and improve the Markdown style. Do not use any external services.
evals.expected_output: Does not trigger markdown.new because the task is local Markdown editing with no need for a public URL, file upload, crawl, or markdown.new-specific behavior.

assertions.text: Does not route to markdown.new for purely local Markdown editing

evals.name: searched-python-docs-page
evals.prompt: I found https://docs.python.org/3/tutorial/index.html from a web search. Use markdown.new to convert it and tell me which response shape is safest if I need to inspect the title, method, token count, and content separately.
evals.expected_output: Routes to the URL conversion guidance, recommends JSON output, and treats the Python docs page as a clean public docs-page example that markdown.new handles well.

assertions.text: Routes to references/url-conversion.md for a searched docs page
Recommends GET with ?format=json or POST / for structured output fields

evals.name: searched-mdn-reference-page
evals.prompt: I found https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Fetch_API in search results. Show me how to run it through markdown.new and what metadata fields I should expect back.
evals.expected_output: Uses markdown.new's single-page conversion flow, treats the MDN page as a handled public reference page, and mentions fields like success, url, title, content, method, duration_ms, and tokens when available.

assertions.text: Treats the MDN page as a standard URL conversion case
Mentions that metadata fields beyond content are available in JSON mode

evals.name: searched-public-pdf-file
evals.prompt: I found this public PDF in search results: https://ontheline.trincoll.edu/images/bookdown/sample-local-pdf.pdf. Use markdown.new on it and tell me whether to treat it as page conversion or file conversion.
evals.expected_output: Routes to the file conversion guidance, treats the PDF as a remote file URL, and prefers JSON output if the caller wants metadata plus content.

assertions.text: Routes to references/file-conversion.md for a searched PDF URL
Treats a direct PDF URL as a file conversion case, not a normal HTML page

evals.name: searched-public-csv-file
evals.prompt: I found this CSV from a web search: https://web.stanford.edu/class/archive/cs/cs109/cs109.1166/stuff/titanic.csv. Show me the markdown.new path that should handle it best and what shape of result to expect.
evals.expected_output: Routes to file conversion guidance, treats the CSV as a remote file URL, and notes that tokens may be missing even when the conversion succeeds.

assertions.text: Routes to references/file-conversion.md for a searched CSV URL
Mentions that some file conversions succeed without a tokens field

evals.name: searched-raw-github-markdown-file
evals.prompt: Search surfaced this raw GitHub file URL: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aurule/json-kv/master/README.md. Use markdown.new on it and explain why this is better than a normal GitHub blob page.
evals.expected_output: Uses markdown.new file conversion or URL conversion successfully on the raw file URL and explicitly contrasts it with the bad blob-URL pattern.

assertions.text: Explains that raw GitHub URLs work better than blob URLs
Routes to references/file-conversion.md or references/gotchas.md

evals.name: searched-large-react-reference-page
evals.prompt: I found https://react.dev/reference/react/useEffect from search. Use markdown.new if it makes sense, but tell me if the output is likely to be too large to hand straight to another agent.
evals.expected_output: Acknowledges that markdown.new can convert the page, but warns that large successful conversions can still be operationally awkward and may need trimming or a narrower URL.

assertions.text: Recognizes large successful conversions as a separate risk
Routes to references/url-conversion.md and references/gotchas.md

evals.name: searched-huge-wikipedia-page
evals.prompt: A search result gave me https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alan_Turing. If I run that through markdown.new, should I use the result directly or do something more careful?
evals.expected_output: Explains that the page is technically convertible but potentially enormous, recommends a more targeted URL or a summarization/slicing step, and treats this as a large-page gotcha rather than a hard failure.

assertions.text: Warns that a huge page can be a bad direct input even when conversion succeeds
Does not present the Wikipedia page as a simple happy-path case without caveats
